result:
No macro overrides found — all four are genuine (inline) function calls. cbm's graph had no useful `CALLS` edges for this function (file/function nodes returned empty), so I verified directly from source, which is authoritative here.

## `ksys_read()` — fs/read_write.c:602-619

Functions directly called, in source order:

| Line | Call | Nature |
|---|---|---|
| 604 | `fdget_pos(fd)` | `static inline` function, `include/linux/file.h:72` — internally calls `__fdget_pos()` (extern), but that's a nested call inside `fdget_pos`, not a direct call from `ksys_read`. |
| 608 | `file_ppos(f.file)` | `static inline` function defined just above in the same file, `fs/read_write.c:597` — returns `&file->f_pos` or `NULL` for stream files. |
| 613 | `vfs_read(f.file, buf, count, ppos)` | Ordinary (non-inline) function, declared in `include/linux/fs.h`. |
| 616 | `fdput_pos(f)` | `static inline` function, `include/linux/file.h:77` — internally may call `__f_unlock_pos()`, again nested, not direct. |

No macros are involved in any of these four call sites — `fdget_pos`, `file_ppos`, `vfs_read`, and `fdput_pos` are all real (inline or normal) C functions, not `#define`d macros (confirmed via grep, no `#define` exists for any of these names).

Other appearances of `ksys_read` in the file (line 623, inside `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, ...)`) are a caller of `ksys_read`, not something it calls.
